channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: How I Refactored My Widget Embed Code: From 8 Lines of JavaScript to a Single Data Attribute
item.description: I've simplified the embed code for telegram chat widget by pasting just one line. Here is the technical journey behind it. After creating a chat widget JavaScript file, the embed code looked like...

item.title: Prove You're 18 Without Showing Who You Are: The Cryptography Big Tech Won't Use
item.description: Engineering a 'Yes/No' without the 'Who/Where' The technical landscape of age verification is undergoing a fundamental shift that every computer vision developer needs to track. We are moving away...

item.title: Using Git Commits as Claude Code's Memory
item.description: Claude Code's context window is a liability. The longer a session runs, the more you're paying to re-read your own conversation history — and the more likely the model drifts on earlier context....

item.title: The no BS guide to show your impact with XYZ Framework
item.description: How many times have you lost a promotion because you weren't able to describe your contributions to a project? Or even worse, has it never happened that you couldn't quickly present the impact you...

item.title: Claude! Stop Burning Tokens on Your Agent's Tool Output!
item.description: A Two-Stage Curator That Pays for Itself I watched Claude Code feed 108,894 bytes of seq 1 20000 back into its own context window. That output contained 20,000 integers. No errors. No signal. No...

item.title: From Personal Project to Engineering Lesson: What 40 Dev.to Posts Taught Me About Building Software
item.description: From Personal Project to Engineering Lesson: What 40 Dev.to Posts Taught Me About Building Software Honestly, I never thought I'd write 40 articles about the same project. Here I am, two years and...

item.title: Building the Future: The Journey of Kamsi Ibeawuchi
item.description: When I first started coding, I thought it would be straightforward. Learn the syntax, understand the logic, and build something cool. It didn’t happen that way. I’m Kamsi Ibeawuchi, a Software...

item.title: 7 Hidden Uses of Context Engineering You Probably Didn't Know in 2026

item.title: Effective Request Cancellation with Axios in JavaScript
item.description: Originally published on PEAKIQ Key Points: CancelTokens in Axios: Axios allows us to create CancelTokens using the CancelToken.source() method. These tokens can be included in requests to enable...

item.title: The Missing Layer in Every AI Agent Framework Comparison
item.description: Every comparison of AI agent frameworks evaluates features, APIs, and ease of use. But they all miss one thing: what happens when you need to run agents from different frameworks together. The...

item.title: Microsoft Teams is trying to fix accidental hand-raising
item.description: If you've ever had the embarrassing displeasure of interrupting a Microsoft Teams meeting by accidentally hitting the "raise hand" feature, then I have some good news for you. Microsoft is...

item.title: Ikea and Samsung promise glitch-free SmartThings integration
item.description: Ikea's two dozen Matter-over-Thread devices have proved problematic since launch, but Samsung says its SmartThings platform now has it all sorted. If true, it means Ikea's sensors and complex...

item.title: Apple Sports now lets you follow scores in a CarPlay widget
item.description: I was disappointed when Apple launched a new Sports iOS widget last year without CarPlay support, but I'm pleased to report that's changing this week. The latest Apple Sports update includes...

item.title: PlayStation’s age-gating restrictions are coming to UK consoles
item.description: Sony is starting to comply with the UK's Online Safety Act by notifying PlayStation players of its age verification requirements today. PS4 and PS5 owners in the UK and Ireland won't be able to...

item.title: CISA Adds 8 Exploited Flaws to KEV, Sets April-May 2026 Federal Deadlines
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Monday added eight new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, including three flaws impacting...

item.title: Dyson’s back with a travel-size Supersonic hairdryer
item.description: Ten years ago, Dyson kicked off the hair gadget arms race with its $400 Supersonic hairdryer. Today, it's back with a slightly smaller and cheaper travel-size version. As the name suggests, the...

item.title: How to Ground a Korean AI Agent in Real Demographics with Synthetic Personas

item.description: Though Tim Cook is shedding his CEO title for the role of Apple's executive chairman, it appears he'll keep one of his most important duties: that of the company's Trump whisperer. "As executive...

item.title: Anthropic takes $5B from Amazon and pledges $100B in cloud spending in return
item.description: Amazon has made another circular AI deal: It's investing another $5 billion in Anthropic. Anthropic has agreed to spend $100 billion on AWS in return.

item.title: The Lenovo Legion Go S is RAMageddon’s latest victim
item.description: You can still find the Asus Xbox Ally X and the MSI Claw 8 AI Plus for $999 and $1,049 respectively, but Lenovo's Legion Go S has seemingly given up the fight. The best version of Lenovo's 8-inch...

item.title: A Humanoid Robot Set a Half-Marathon Record in China
item.description: An autonomous robot from the company Honor ran a half marathon in 50:26, beating the human record by 7 minutes.

item.title: Google rolls out Gemini in Chrome in 7 new countries
item.description: Google is rolling out Gemini in Chrome in Australia, Indonesia, Japan, the Philippines, Singapore, South Korea, and Vietnam. The company is rolling this feature out to both desktop and iOS in all...

item.title: John Ternus is taking over from Tim Cook as Apple’s CEO
item.description: The multitrillion-dollar home of the iPhone, Mac, and so many other tech gadgets is getting a new leader this fall, as Apple has announced that Tim Cook will be replaced as CEO on September 1st by...

item.title: Read Tim Cook’s letter to the Apple world as he departs as CEO
item.description: Apple CEO Tim Cook has written a letter to the community as he prepares to step down from his role in September. "This is not goodbye. But at this moment of transition, I wanted to take the...

item.title: Apple names Johny Srouji as chief hardware officer
item.description: Apple has appointed Johny Srouji as its new chief hardware officer, "effective immediately," according to an announcement on Monday. He is stepping into the shoes of current hardware engineering...

item.title: It’s not just one thing — it’s another thing
item.description: This sentence construction ("It's not just this — it's that") has become so common in AI-generated writing that it's no longer just a clue that a piece of writing may be synthetic — it's almost a...

item.title: SGLang CVE-2026-5760 (CVSS 9.8) Enables RCE via Malicious GGUF Model Files
item.description: A critical security vulnerability has been disclosed in SGLang that, if successfully exploited, could result in remote code execution on susceptible systems. The vulnerability, tracked as...

item.title: NSA spies are reportedly using Anthropic’s Mythos, despite Pentagon feud
item.description: NSA is said to be using Anthropic's restricted Mythos AI model.

item.title: CEO and CFO suddenly depart AI nuclear power upstart Fermi
item.description: The startup, co-founded by former U.S. Energy Secretary Rick Perry, has faced headwinds with its AI campus in Texas.

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Vercel Hack, Push Fraud, QEMU Abused, New Android RATs Emerge & More
item.description: Monday’s recap shows the same pattern in different places. A third-party tool becomes a way in, then leads to internal access. A trusted download path is briefly swapped to deliver malware....
